data.title: SK Telecom Moves to Fully Acquire SK Broadband via Cash Share Exchange
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Sk Telecom ( (SKM) ) has provided an announcement.

On March 26, 2026, SK Telecom’s board approved a cash-based share exchange to acquire all remaining shares of its fixed-line unit SK Broadband, turning it into a wholly owned subsidiary. The transaction values SK Broadband minority shares at 15,032 won per common share, with a share exchange ratio set at 1 SK Telecom to 0.1916882 SK Broadband, based on Korean securities law valuation methods.

SK Telecom will use its reference market price of 78,419 won per share, which exceeds its asset value, while SK Broadband’s price is derived from intrinsic value combining asset and profit metrics. An external appraisal by Deloitte Anjin, completed between March 3 and March 26, 2026, deemed the exchange ratio appropriate, and SK Telecom expects full ownership to improve management efficiency, enhance strategic flexibility and business synergies, and ultimately boost overall corporate value.

The most recent analyst rating on (SKM) stock is a Buy with a $31.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sk Telecom stock, see the SKM Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SKM Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SKM is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held back by the sharp profitability and return deterioration and a cautious earnings-call outlook tied to cybersecurity-related impacts, penalties, and subscriber pressure. Solid cash generation and a generally supported balance sheet help offset these risks, while technicals are mixed (short-term weakness but longer-term support) and valuation looks demanding given the earnings compression despite a moderate dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on SKM stock, click here.

More about Sk Telecom

SK Telecom Co., Ltd. is a leading South Korean telecommunications operator, historically focused on mobile services and increasingly active across converged connectivity and digital platforms. Its subsidiary SK Broadband Co., Ltd. provides fixed-line telecommunications services, positioning the group as an integrated provider across wireless and wireline networks in the Korean market.

Average Trading Volume: 1,656,721

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: $11.18B

See more insights into SKM stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

The latest announcement is out from Minaean SP Construction ( (TSE:PWER) ).

Global Power Solutions Corp. has joined the Canada Data Centres’ Alliance, aligning its hydrogen-based decentralized power solutions with the growing needs of Canada’s data centre and AI infrastructure market. The move positions the company within a key industry forum focused on addressing power availability, grid constraints, lower-carbon energy integration and national infrastructure policy coordination.

To bolster its market visibility, the company has also signed a two-month, $50,000 marketing agreement with Machai Capital Inc. for digital marketing and investor awareness services, subject to TSX Venture Exchange approval. This campaign is intended to enhance Global Power Solutions’ profile among investors and stakeholders as it pivots further into clean energy and digital infrastructure-related opportunities.

data.title: Wolfram Resources Reschedules AGM After Lack of Quorum
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Becket Invest PLC ( (GB:WFR) ) has issued an update.

Wolfram Resources PLC has disclosed that its adjourned Annual General Meeting, scheduled for 11:00 a.m. on 26 March 2026, was opened but could not proceed because a quorum of shareholders was not present. In response, the meeting was promptly adjourned and rescheduled to reconvene at the offices of AlbR Capital Limited in London at 3:00 p.m. on 31 March 2026.

The company confirmed that all proxy votes already submitted for the AGM resolutions will remain valid for the reconvened session, ensuring previously expressed shareholder preferences are preserved. The adjournment highlights a temporary governance delay but indicates an intention to complete the formal approval process once sufficient shareholder participation is secured, with further updates promised as necessary.

More about Becket Invest PLC

Wolfram Resources PLC is a U.K.-based company, with its shares listed in London, though the specific industry and product focus are not detailed in the announcement. The company holds its corporate meetings in the City of London, suggesting a capital markets-oriented shareholder base and governance framework aligned with U.K. corporate standards.

Average Trading Volume: 1,721,144

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

For a thorough assessment of WFR stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

STS Global Income & Growth Trust PLC ( (GB:STS) ) has shared an announcement.

STS Global Income & Growth Trust PLC has repurchased 50,000 of its own ordinary shares at 219.50 pence per share, with the shares to be held in treasury rather than cancelled. This transaction forms part of the trust’s capital management activities and may support its share price and net asset value per share over time.

Following the buyback, the company’s total issued share capital stands at 175,188,185 shares, of which 61,127,770 are held in treasury, leaving 114,060,415 shares carrying voting rights. The updated voting rights figure provides a new reference point for shareholders to assess and report any changes in their ownership stakes under FCA transparency rules.

data.title: Winpak Names Veteran Executive David Johns as Interim CEO Amid Leadership Transition
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Winpak ( (TSE:WPK) ).

Winpak Ltd. has appointed longtime executive David A. Johns as interim president and chief executive officer, effective immediately, marking the return of a leader who joined the company in 1987 and previously ran its largest division before retiring in 2017. Johns’ appointment follows the immediate departure of former CEO Olivier Muggli, as the board continues work on a succession plan for a permanent chief executive.

The company is also expanding the role of vice president and chief financial officer Scott Taylor, who has been with Winpak since 1999 and became CFO in 2022, to support Johns during this transition. By leaning on two seasoned insiders with extensive knowledge of day-to-day operations, Winpak is signaling an emphasis on stability and operational continuity at a time of leadership change, which should help reassure employees, investors, and other stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:WPK) stock is a Hold with a C$52.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Winpak stock, see the TSE:WPK Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on WPK Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, WPK is a Outperform.

The score is primarily supported by strong financial resilience (minimal leverage and steady profitability). Technicals add support via a clear uptrend and positive momentum, though the high Stochastic suggests limited near-term room. Valuation is reasonable on earnings, but the low dividend yield modestly tempers the overall score.

To see Spark’s full report on WPK stock, click here.

More about Winpak

Winpak Ltd., listed on the TSX under the symbol WPK, operates in the manufacturing sector with a focus on packaging solutions, and its largest and most successful business unit is the Winpak Division. The company relies on a long-tenured management team with deep operational and financial expertise, reflecting a strategy centered on continuity and institutional knowledge in its leadership ranks.

data.title: FPX Nickel and JOGMEC Outline Multiple New Awaruite Targets at Klow Property
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An update from FPX Nickel ( (TSE:FPX) ) is now available.

FPX Nickel has reported new exploration results from the Klow property in British Columbia, where a 2025 rock and stream sediment sampling program, fully funded by Japan’s JOGMEC, has delineated three additional awaruite nickel target zones. The work expands the known awaruite footprint to four large-scale targets within the 164-square-kilometre property, located along trend from FPX’s Baptiste Nickel Project and supported by nearby road and rail infrastructure.

Sampling returned Davis Tube Recoverable nickel values of up to 0.09% across multiple zones, including the Klow, Upper Klow and Mesa targets, with data indicating broad areas of anomalous nickel that are being advanced toward drill-ready status. FPX and JOGMEC have extended their earn-in agreement on Klow to March 31, 2028, and plan focused follow-up sampling in 2026, underscoring ongoing strategic interest in securing new nickel sources and potentially enhancing FPX’s position in the emerging awaruite nickel space.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:FPX) stock is a Hold with a C$0.62 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on FPX Nickel stock, see the TSE:FPX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FPX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FPX is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (pre-revenue losses and ongoing cash burn) despite a strong low-debt balance sheet. Technicals are supportive with the stock above major moving averages and positive MACD, and recent corporate events are favorable (buyback renewal and ESG/partnership progress). Valuation remains constrained by negative earnings and no dividend.

To see Spark’s full report on FPX stock, click here.

More about FPX Nickel

FPX Nickel Corp. is a Canadian mineral exploration company focused on discovering and advancing large-scale nickel projects in British Columbia. Its portfolio includes the flagship Baptiste Nickel Project and the district-scale Klow property, both targeting awaruite-hosted nickel mineralization to supply future nickel demand, particularly for stainless steel and battery-related markets.

data.title: Global Acquisitions Extends CEO Contract, Adopts 2026 Equity Plan
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Global Acquisitions Corporation ( (AASP) ) is now available.

Global Acquisitions Corporation entered into an executive employment agreement with Chief Executive Officer Ronald S. Boreta, dated March 25, 2026 and effective March 1, 2026, extending his leadership for five years with automatic one-year renewals. The pact sets a $270,000 base salary with automatic 10% annual increases, a $250,000 cash sign-on bonus that remains unpaid, potential cash and equity bonuses, strict non-compete and non-solicitation covenants, and a detailed severance and change-of-control package that could significantly increase Boreta’s payout if he is terminated without cause or resigns for good reason.

The agreement outlines substantial acceleration of vesting and cash severance in cases of death, disability, good‑reason resignation, termination without cause or certain change-of-control events, reinforcing Boreta’s job security while limiting his ability to compete or solicit staff and customers for 12 months post‑employment. On March 23, 2026, the board also adopted the Agassi Sports Entertainment Corp. 2026 Equity Incentive Plan, authorizing 1.5 million shares for a broad range of stock-based awards to employees, directors and consultants, which strengthens the company’s ability to use equity compensation for retention and alignment but may dilute existing shareholders as awards are granted.

The 2026 Plan, administered by the board or a compensation committee, permits nonqualified stock options, restricted stock, RSUs, stock appreciation rights, performance awards and other equity-linked grants with market-based exercise pricing and flexible vesting terms. It includes customary provisions for corporate transactions and changes in control, allowing for assumption, substitution or accelerated vesting of awards, and gives the administrator repricing and redesign authority, thereby providing a powerful capital markets and incentive tool that could influence the firm’s cost of compensation and ownership structure over time.

More about Global Acquisitions Corporation

Global Acquisitions Corporation, operating through Agassi Sports Entertainment Corp., appears focused on developing and operating sports and entertainment-related businesses. The company uses equity-based compensation to attract and retain executives, employees, directors and consultants, signaling a growth-oriented corporate strategy and reliance on stock incentives to align stakeholder interests.

Average Trading Volume: 2,365

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $56.4M
